name: Kfc's Honey BBQ Chicken Wings
recipeIngredient: 4 pieces CHICKEN, skin/bone removed and cut into bite-size pieces
2 large EGGS mixed with 1 TBS WATER (or about ½ cup egg substitute--minus the water) or 3 large egg whites mixed with the water
1 bottle KC MASTERPIECE ORIGINAL BBQ SAUCE (use your favorite local brand if you can't find KC's)
1/4 cup HONEY (or to taste--see comments below)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a large skillet or use your deep fryer. Heat oil until it's very hot (around 350-degrees). See comments, below.
2. Wash the chicken wings and remove the wing tips (save for soup or trash); cut the other two pieces in half. Shake off excess water. If desired, use boneless/skinless chicken breasts and cut into bite-size pieces.
3. In a bowl, mix whole eggs or egg whites with 1 Tbsp water until well combined. Set aside.
4. Mix BBQ sauce and honey, tasting as you go. Adjust sweetness to preference. Use Clover Honey or your preferred type.
5. Place flour in a shallow dish. Shake wings in the egg wash, then toss back into the flour to lightly coat. Ensure a heavy coating for better sauce adhesion.
6. Fry wings until golden brown (not fully cooked). Drain on paper towels.
7. Preheat oven to 325-degrees.
8. Once fried and drained, dip each piece into BBQ sauce/honey mixture and place onto baking sheets, spacing pieces apart.
9. Bake for 15-20 minutes or until no longer shiny and juices run clear when pierced with a knife.
This recipe aims to replicate KFC's Honey BBQ Chicken Wings using the specified ingredients and instructions.
